extraction.one_line_summary: VERNON Ethan won the sprint after ALMEIDA João gained one second on QUINTANA at the intermediate sprint with 17km to go, taking the race lead.
tactical_patterns: UAE Emirates prioritized intermediate sprint positioning over stage win support, placing ALMEIDA third at 17km sprint to gain race lead.
Quick-Step flooded late positioning without dedicated leadout train, relying on Vernon's individual speed after depleted sprinter field thinned domestiques.
JOHANNESSON from Xpro aggressively contested intermediate sprint despite GC irrelevance, disrupting expected UAE-Movistar battle dynamics.

rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: Showed tactical awareness to contest intermediate sprint at 17km while race co-leader QUINTANA missed the move completely.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Failed to gain bonus seconds at decisive intermediate sprint despite Movistar positioning, finishing fourth when three seconds available.
rider_signals.VERNON Ethan: positive: Won without traditional leadout despite Quick-Step's sprint train reputation, suggesting individual speed advantage over depleted field.
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: neutral: BikeExchange executed textbook final corner positioning but GROVES couldn't convert setup into win against Vernon's speed.
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: negative: Forced to sprint solo after leadout failed, faded to second despite better positioning than Vernon entering finale.
